titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Harold Igo papers
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday January 23, 2021 at 04:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Dorothy L. Rogers, 2014 and 2016.
November 2016 acquisition, gift of David M. Rodgers, 2016.

bioghist.head: Harold Igo
bioghist.p: Harold Igo (1892-1976) was a writer, newspaper reporter, and playwright. Igo was born in New Florence, Pennsylvania and graduated from Westminster College with a Bachelor of Philosophy in 1915. Igo served overseas in the Army in 1918, and when he returned, decided to become a journalist. He organized the Rio News Syndicate to cover South America for a group of small town North American newspapers and worked for eight years as a reporter. In 1926, he founded the Youngstown Theater, and from 1931-1932 studied play writing and directing at Yale University. From 1933 to 1948, Igo resided in Yellow Springs, Ohio with his wife Sara McKnight Igo. During this time he wrote a number of plays, including American Mass , Coopy When , Ohio Doom , Steel , and Remember Bud Jones . Igo was the founding director of The Island Players Community Theater in Anna Maria Island in 1949, until his retirement in 1966.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Harold Igo Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Harold Igo Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature. Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: I. June 2014 acquisition, 1910 - 1996. II. October 2016 acquisition, 1970 - 1979. III. November 2016 acquisition, 1950 - 1989.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains writings, correspondence, notebooks, photographs, scrapbooks, and other papers by or related to American writer, newspaper reporter, and playwright Harold Igo.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into the following series: I. Correspondence, 1931-1976, II. Plays, 1926-1975, III. Professional papers, 1918 - 1996, IV. Personal papers, 1910 - 1984.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material includes correspondence, writings and play drafts by Igo, and personal and professional papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Igo's wife, Sara McKnight (1902-1998), McKnight's family correspondence, and correspondence with Indian novelist and short story writer Gurbaksh Singh.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typed drafts of plays written by Igo. Drafts includes plays with corresponding notes, and drafts with annotations.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by title, with early plays located at the end of the series.

title.part: Had Lincoln Laughed: A Drama of Exile
Is Saturn Open on Saturday Night?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes, Had Lincoln Laughed: A Play in Four Acts , March Malen: A Folk Play of the Steel Mills , Maude Martin , The Wimple , Crop Over Jump Up , and Devil's Acre: A Drama of the Mills in Three Acts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Igo's writing works and notes. Writings include short stories, poems, notes, play drafts, and song lyrics.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: "Biographical": correspondence, notes, play draft and poems
Notes, poems, short stories, and correspondence
Play drafts, poems, and short stories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the short stories, "An Agent of Truman," "Four Strikes and You're Out!," and "The Lightnin' Bug Special."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stories include "The Day Harry Thaw Met Carrie Nation," "The Game We Almost Didn't Win," and "I Remember Harry."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by folder title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs, scrapbooks, clippings, and other personal papers. Scrapbooks include clippings and other material related to Yellow Springs, Ohio, photographs, clippings, and programs related to The Island Players Community Theater, and other material related to Igo's plays, including clippings, programs, and photographs.

did.unittitle: Funeral guest book for Harold Igo
Photographs with writing by George Santayana

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This scrapbook contains an image of the racist practice of yellowface, the portrayal of East Asians by white actors.

did.unittitle: Notebook featuring writings, notes, and clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a biographical statement, summaries of Igo's plays, and poems by Igo.
